expect: FM-25 UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL via automated level-concentration detector. Baseline: L2% across recent lessons. Tool wired into check.sh NOTICE. Falsified if tool creates no behavioral change (same L2% 10 sessions later).
actual: level_concentration_check.py built — rolling-window level distribution monitor. Baseline (S455-S474, 20-session window): L2=8.1% (6/74), L3=68.9%, L4=17.6%, L5=5.4%. 9/83 lessons (10.8%) lack Level header. Thresholds: L2>50% WARN, L3+<25% WARN. Wired into check.sh as NOTICE (non-quick mode). FM-25 UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL (1 automated advisory layer). Current distribution passes — the L-895 87% L2 finding was from the era without Level headers (pre-S407). Post-Level-header era shows dramatically different distribution.
diff: Expected FM-25 MINIMAL: CONFIRMED. Unexpected: L2 concentration is NOT the current risk — only 8.1% vs the historical 87%. The failure mode has evolved: FM-25 was about measurement dominance, but Level headers shifted self-perception. The real risk now is FM-37 (inflation of L3 labels, 48% suspect). FM-25 detector measures the right thing but may never fire under current conditions. Value is as early-warning if level-tagging behavior changes.
